title: Streaming Analysis, Chunk Boundaries, and Resource Budgets · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Bookbot run contains one plausible path, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, reporting, CLI, testing, performance, packaging, security, or release defect; identify the first failed boundary before changing code. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded streaming analyzer and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Process lines or bounded chunks incrementally and keep only state required by the declared report.
Carry a bounded token fragment across chunk boundaries and prove concatenated-chunk equivalence.
Use an incremental decoder or text stream that preserves multibyte sequences and explicit error policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Batch Stream Equivalence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence-1beye8" that uses case 7945 to prove streaming and batch equivalence under token-fragment and peak-memory bounds. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Batch Stream Equivalence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Batch Stream Equivalence into observable performance evidence. For Bookbot and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Batch Stream Equivalence, keep Batch Stream Equivalence connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence-1beye8
# Competency: bookbot-batch-stream-equivalence.
# Case 7945: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy.
def evidence_bookbot_batch_stream_equivalence_1beye8(chunks: int = 8, batch_tokens: int = 120, stream_tokens: int = 120, peak_bytes: int = 8192, memory_budget: int = 16384, fragments_closed: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1beye8 = "1beye8-8eyeb1-7945"
    assert evidence_variant_1beye8
    evidence_axes_1beye8 = (
        "corpus1beye8",
        "policy8eyeb1",
        "revision7945",
        "command1beye87945",
        "report8eyeb17945",
        "test79451beye8",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1beye8)) == 6
    if chunks < 1 or batch_tokens < 0 or stream_tokens < 0:
        return False, stream_tokens, "stream-shape"
    if stream_tokens != batch_tokens:
        return False, stream_tokens, "batch-stream-drift"
    if peak_bytes < 0 or peak_bytes > memory_budget:
        return False, peak_bytes, "memory-budget"
    if not fragments_closed:
        return False, chunks, "partial-token"
    assert peak_bytes <= memory_budget
    return True, stream_tokens, "bounded-stream"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.
